question: Write the Following Ratio in the Reduced Form. The ratio of diagonal to the length of a rectangle, having length 4 cm and breadth 3 cm.
answer:
Length of the rectangle, l = 4 cm
Breadth of the rectangle, b = 3 cm
Now,
Diagonal of the rectangle = √ [l² + b²]
= √ [ 3² + 4²]
 = √ [ 9 + 16]
= √25
= 5 cm
∴ Ratio of diagonal to the length of the rectangle = Diagonal of the rectangle : Length of the rectangle = 5 cm : 4 cm = 5 : 4.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Write the following ratio in the reduced form. The ratio of perimeter to the area of a square, having side 4 cm.
answer:
Side of the square = 4 cm
Perimeter of the square = 4 × Side of the square = 4 × 4 cm = 16 cm
Area of the square = (Side of the square)2 = (4 cm)2 = 16 cm2
∴ Ratio of perimeter to area of the square = Perimeter of the square : Area of the square = 16 : 16 = 1 : 1
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Check whether the following numbers are in continued proportion. 2, 4, 8
answer:
The numbers a, b, c are in continued proportion if a/b = b/c.
2/4 = 1/2
4/8 = 1/2
Since 2/4 = 4/8, so the numbers 2, 4, 8 are in continued proportion.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Check whether the following numbers are in continued proportion. 1, 2, 3
answer: Since 1/2 ≠ 2/3, so the numbers 1, 2, 3 are not in continued proportion.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Check whether the following numbers are in continued proportion. 9, 12, 16
answer:
The numbers a, b, c are in continued proportion if a/b = b/c
9/12 = 3/4
12/16 = 3/4
Since 9/12 = 12/16, so the numbers 9, 12, 16 are in continued proportion.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Check whether the following numbers are in continued proportion. 3, 5, 8
answer: Since 3/5 ≠ 5/8, so the numbers 3, 5, 8 are not in continued proportion.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: A, b, c are in continued proportion. If a = 3 and c = 27 then find b.
answer:
It is given that 3, b, 27 are in continued proportion.
therefore 3/b = b/27
⇒ b² = 3 xx 27
⇒ b² = 81
⇒ b = √81 = 9
Thus, the value of b is 9.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Write the ratio of first quantity to second quantity in the reduced form. 17 Rupees, 25 Rupees 60 paise
answer:
17 Rupees = 1700 paise (Rs 1 = 100 paise)
25 Rupees 60 paise = 25 Rupees + 60 paise = 2500 paise + 60 paise = 2560 paise
∴ Ratio of 17 Rupees to 25 Rupees 60 paise
= 1700 paise : 2560 paise
= 1700/2560
= [1700 ÷ 20]/[ 2560 ÷ 20] ...(HCF of 1700 and 2560 = 20)
= 85/128
= 85 : 128
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Write the ratio of first quantity to second quantity in the reduced form. 5 dozen, 120 units
answer:
Ratio of 5 dozen to 120 units
= 5 × 12 units : 120 units
= 60 units : 120 units
= 60/120
= 1/2
= 1 : 2
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Write the ratio of first quantity to second quantity in the reduced form. 4 sq.m, 800 sq.cm
answer:
1 "sq.m"² = 10000 "sq.cm"²
4 "sq.m"² = 4 xx 10000 "sq.cm"² = 40000 "sq.cm"²
therefore Ratio of 4 "sq.m"² to 800 "sq.cm"²
= 40000 "sq.cm"² : 800 "sq.cm"²
= 40000 /800
= 50
= 50 : 1
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If ab=23 then find the value of the following expression. 4a+3b3b
answer:
a/b = 2/3
⇒ [4a]/[3b] = [4 xx 2]/[ 3 xx 3] = 8/9
Applying componendo, we get
[ 4a + 3b]/(3b) = [ 8 + 9]/9
⇒ [ 4a + 3b]/(3b) = [ 17 ]/9
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If ab=23 then find the value of the following expression. 5a2+2b25a2-2b2
answer:
a/b = 2/3
Squaring both sides,
⇒ a²/b² = 4/9 
⇒ (5a²)/(2b²) = [5 xx 4]/[2 xx 9]
⇒ (5a²)/(2b²) = 20/18 
Applying componendo and dividendo, we get
[5a² + 2b²]/[ 5a² - 2b²] = [20+18]/[20-18]
⇒ [5a² + 2b²]/[ 5a² - 2b²] = [38]/[2]
⇒ [5a² + 2b²]/[ 5a² - 2b²] = 19
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If abab=23 then find the values of the following expression a3+b3b3
answer:
a/b = 2/3
∴ a³/b³ = 8/27 ...(Cubing both sides)
Applying componendo, we get
[a³ + b³]/b³ = [8 + 27]/27 
[a³ + b³]/b³ = 35/27
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If ab=23 then find the value of the following expression. 7b-4a7b+4a
answer:
a/b = 2/3
Applying invertendo, we get
b/a = 3/2
= [7b]/[ 4a] = [ 7 xx 3]/[ 4 xx 2] = 21/8
Applying componendo and dividendo, we get
=[ 7b + 4a ]/[ 7b - 4a ] = [ 21 + 8 ]/[ 21 - 8]
=[ 7b + 4a ]/[ 7b - 4a ] = 29/13
Applying invertendo, we get
=[ 7b - 4a ]/[ 7b + 4a ] = 13/29
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If a, b, c, d are in proportion, then prove that 11a2+9ac11b2+9bd=a2+3acb2+3bd
answer:
It is given that a, b, c, d are in proportion.
therefore a/b = c/d = k
⇒ a = bk, c = dk
∴ [ 11a² + 9ac]/[ 11b² + 9bd]
= [11(bk)² + 9 xx bk xx dk]/[ 11b² +9bd]
= [k²(11b² + 9bd)]/[11b² + 9bd]
= k² ...(1)
∴ [a² + 3ac]/[ b² + 3bd]
= [(bk)² + 3 xx bk xx dk]/[ b² + 3bd]
= [k²( b² + 3bd)]/[b² + 3bd ]
= k² ...(2)
From (1) and (2), we get
[ 11a² + 9ac]/[ 11b² + 9bd] = [ a² + 3ac]/[ b² + 3bd]
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If a, b, c, d are in proportion, then prove that a2+5c2b2+5d2=ab
answer:
It is given that a, b, c, d are in proportion.
therefore a/b = c/d = k
⇒ a = bk, c = dk
√[( a² + 5c²)/( b² + 5d²)]
= √[(bk)² + 5(dk)²]/(b² + 5d² )
= √[k²( b² + 5d²)]/(b² + 5d²)
= √(k²)
= k ...(1)
a/b = [bk]/b = k ...(2)
From (1) and (2), we get
√([a² + 5c²]/[b² + 5d²]) = a/b
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If a, b, c, d are in proportion, then prove that a2+ab+b2a2-ab+b2=c2+cd+d2c2-cd+d2
answer:
It is given that a, b, c, d are in proportion.
therefore a/b = c/d = k
⇒ a = bk , c = dk
LHS = [a² + ab + b²]/[a² - ab + b²]
= [(bk)² + bk xx b + b²]/[(bk)² - bk xx b + b²]
= [b²(k² + k + 1)]/[b²(k² + k + 1 )]
= [ k²+k+1 ]/[ k² - k +1 ] ....(1)
RHS = [c² + cd + d² ]/[ c² - cd + d² ]
= [(dk)² + dk xx d + d²]/[(dk)² - dk xx d + d² ]
= [d²(k² + k + 1)]/[d²(k² - k + 1 )]
= [k²+k+1]/[k² - k +1] ...(2)
LHS = RHS
From (1) and (2), we get
[a² + ab + b²]/[a² - ab + b²] = [c² + cd + d² ]/[ c² - cd + d² ]
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If a, b, c are in continued proportion, then prove that aa+2b=a-2ba-4c
answer:
, b, c are in continued proportion.
therefore a/b = b/c = k
⇒ a = bk, b = ck
⇒ a = bk = ck xx k = ck²
LHS = a/[ a + 2b] = [ck²]/[ ck² + 2 xx ck ]
= [ck²]/[ ck( k + 2) ]
= k/( k + 2) ...(1)
RHS = [ a - 2b]/[ a - 4c ] = [ ck² - 2 xx ck]/[ ck² - 4c ]
= [ck( k -2)]/[c(k² - 4)]
= [k( k -2)]/[(k+2)(k-2)]
= k/(k + 2) ...(2)
From (1) and (2), we get
a/[ a + 2b] = [a - 2b]/[ a - 4c]
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If a, b, c are in continued proportion, then prove that bb+c=a-ba-c
answer:
, b, c are in continued proportion.
therefore a/b = b/c = k
⇒ a = bk, b = ck
⇒ a = bk = ck xx k = ck²
LHS = b/[ b + c] = [ck]/[ ck + c] 
= [ck]/[ c( k + 1)]
= k/( k + 1) ..(1)
RHS = [ a - b]/[ a - c ] = [ ck² - ck]/[ ck² - c ]
= [ck( k -1)]/[c(k² - 1)]
= [k( k -1)]/[(k-1)(k+1)]
= k/(k + 1) ...(2)
From (1) and (2), we get
b/[b+c] = [a-b]/[a-c]
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If 2x-3y3z+y=z-yz-x=x+3z2y-3x then prove that every ratio = xy.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If by+czb2+c2=cz+axc2+a2=ax+bya2+b2 then prove that xa=yb=zc
answer:
[by + cz]/[b² + c²] = [cz + ax]/[c² + a²] = [ax + by]/[a² + b²]
By theorem of equal ratios,
⇒ [ by + cz ]/[ b² + c²] = [ cz + ax ]/[ c² + a²] = [ ax + by ]/[ a² + b² ] = [(by + cz) +(cz + ax) + (ax + by)]/[( b² + c²) + ( c² + a²) + (a² + b² )] 
⇒ [ 2ax + 2by + 2cz ]/[ 2a² + 2b² + 2c²] 
⇒ [ 2(ax + by + cz) ]/[ 2(a² + b² + c²)]
⇒ [(ax + by + cz) ]/[(a² + b² + c²)] 
Again applying theorem of equal ratios, we get
⇒ [( by + cz )- (ax + by + cz)]/[(b² + c²) - (a² + b² + c²)] = [(cz + ax) - (ax + by + cz)]/[(c² + a²) - (a² + b² + c²)] = [(ax + by) - (ax + by + cz)] /[(a² + b²) - (a² + b² + c²)]
⇒ (-ax)/(-a²) = (-by)/(-b²) = (-cz)/(-c²)
⇒ x/a = y/b = z/c
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: By using variables x and y form any five linear equations in two variables.

 x + y= 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equation. x + y = 4 ; 2x - 5y = 1
answer:
1Show Solutionx + y = 4 ...(I)
2x - 5y = 1 ...(II)
From (I) we have
x = 4 - y
Putting this value of x in (II)
2 (4 - y) - 5y = 1
⇒ 8 - 2y - 5y = 1
⇒ 8 - 7y = 1
⇒ 8 - 1 = 7y
⇒ 7 = 7y
⇒ y = 1
Putting the value of y in (I) we have
x + 1 = 4
⇒ x = 3
Thus, (x, y) = (3, 1)
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equation. 2x + y = 5; 3x - y = 5
answer:
1Show Solution2x + y = 5 
∴ y = 5 - 2x ...(I)
3x - y = 5 ...(II)
Substituting (I) in (II)
3x - y = 5
∴ 3x - (5 - 2x) = 5
∴ 3x - 5 + 2x = 5
∴ 5x = 5 + 5
5x = 10
⇒ x = 10/5 = 2 
Putting x = 2 in (I) we have,
2 × 2 + y = 5
⇒ 4 + y = 5
⇒ y = 1
(x, y) = (2, 1)
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equation. 3x - 5y = 16; x - 3y = 8
answer:
1Show Solution3x - 5y = 16 ...(1)
x - 3y = 8 ...(2)
From (2), x = 8 + 3y ...(3)
substituting equation 3 in equation 1, we get
3(8 + 3y) − 5y = 16
24 + 9y − 5y = 16
9y − 5y = 16 − 24
4y = −8
y = (− 8)/4
y = −2
substituting y = −2 in equation 3, we get
x = 8 + 3y
x = 8 + 3 × (−2)
x = 8 − 6
x = 2
Therefore, x = 2 and y = −2.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equations. 2y − x = 0; 10x + 15y = 105
answer:
1Show Solution2y − x = 0
2y = x
∴ x = 2y ...(I)
10x + 15y = 105 ...(II)
Putting x = 2y in equation (II)
10 × 2y + 15y = 105
20y + 15y = 105
35y = 105
y = 105/35
∴ y = 3
Putting y = 3 in equation (I)
x = 2 × 3 = 6
∴ (x, y) = (6, 3)
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equation. 2x + 3y + 4 = 0; x − 5y = 11
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following set of simultaneous equation. 2x - 7y = 7; 3x + y = 22
answer:
1Show Solution2x − 7y = 7 ...(I)
3x + y = 22 ...(II)
y = 22 − 3x ...(III)
Putting y = 22 − 3x in equation (I)
2x − 7(22 − 3x) = 7
2x − 154 + 21x = 7 
23x = 7 + 154
23x = 161
x = 161/23
x = 7
Putting x = 7 in equation (III)
y = 22 − 3 × 7
y = 22 − 21
y = 1
∴ (x, y) = (7, 1)
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: In an envelope there are some 5 rupee notes and some 10 rupee notes. Total amount of these notes together is 350 rupees. Number of 5 rupee notes are less by 10 than twice number of 10 rupee notes.
answer:
Let the number of Rs 5 notes be x and the number of Rs 10 notes be y.
5x + 10y = 350 ...(I) 
Number of 5 rupee notes are less by 10 than twice number of 10 rupee notes
∴ x = 2y – 10
∴ x – 2y = –10 ...(II)
Multiplying equation (II) by 5,
5x – 10y = –50 ...(III)
Adding equations (I) and (III)
 5x + 10y = 350
+ 5x – 10y = –50 
 10x = 300 
∴ x =300/10
∴ x = 30
Substituting x = 30 in equation (II),
x – 2y = –10
30 – 2y = –10
∴ 30 + 10 = 2y
∴ 40 = 2y
∴ y = 40/2
∴ y = 20
Number of Rs 5 notes = 30, number of Rs 10 notes = 20
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The denominator of a fraction is 1 less than twice its numerator. If 1 is added to numerator and denominator respectively, the ratio of numerator to denominator is 3 : 5. Find the fraction.
answer:
Let numerator of fraction be x and its denominator be y.
required fraction = "x"/"y"
y = 2x - 1
2x - y = 1 ...(1)
("x" + 1)/("y" + 1) = 3/5 
 ∴ 5 (x + 1) = 3 (y + 1)
 ∴ 5x + 5 = 3y + 3
 ∴ 5x - 3y = 3 - 5
 ∴ 5x - 3y = - 2 ...(2)
Multiply equation (1) by (3)
6x - 3y = 3 ...(3)
Subtracting equation (2) from (3)
6x − 3y = 3
5x − 3y = −2
− + + 
x = 5
∴ x = 5 
Putting x = 5 in equation (1)
∴ 2 (5) - y = 1
∴ 10 - y = 1
∴ - y = - 9
 y = 9 
∴ required fraction is "x"/"y" = 5/9
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The sum of ages of Priyanka and Deepika is 34 years. Priyanka is elder to Deepika by 6 years. Then find their today's ages.
answer:
Let the age of Priyanka be x years and that of Deepika be y years. 
The sum of ages of Priyanka and Deepika is 34 years.
x + y = 34 ...(I)
Priyanka is elder to Deepika by 6 years.
x - y = 6 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II) we have
 x + y = 34 ...(I)
+ x - y = 6 ...(II)
2x = 40
∴ 2x = 40
⇒ x = 20
Putting the value of x = 20 in (II) we have
x - y = 6
20 - y = 6
20 - 6 = y
y = 14
Thus, Priyanka's age = 20 years and Deepika's age = 14 years.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The total number of lions and peacocks in a certain zoo is 50. The total number of their legs is 140. Then find the number of lions and peacocks in the zoo.
answer:
Let the number of lions be x and the number of peacocks be y. 
The total number of lions and peacocks in a certain zoo is 50. 
So, x + y = 50 ...(I)
The total number of their legs is 140. 
Lion has 4 legs while peacock has 2.
4x + 2y = 140
⇒ 2x + y = 70 ...(II) 
Subtracting (I) from (II) we have
x + y = 50
2x + y = 70
− − − 
−x = −20
∴ x = 20
Putting x = 20 in equation (I)
20 + y = 50
y = 50 - 20
y = 30
Thus, the number of lions = 20 and the number of peacocks = 30.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Sanjay Gets Fixed Monthly Income. Every Year There is a Certain Increment in His Salary.
answer:
Let the fixed monthly income be Rs x. 
Annual increment be Rs y. 
After 4 years, his monthly salary was Rs. 4500 
Monthly salary + annual increment of 4 years = 4500
x + 4y = 4500 ...(I)
After 10 years his monthly salary became 5400 rupees
Monthly salary + annual increment of 10 years = 5400
x + 10y = 5400 ...(II)
Subtracting I from II
x + 4y = 4500
x + 10y = 5400
− − − 
 −6y = −900
∴ y = 150
put y = 150 in equation (I)
x + 4y = 4500
x + 4 × 150 = 4500
x + 600 = 4500
x = 4500 − 600
x = 3900
Thus, the monthly salary = Rs. 3900
Annual increment = Rs.150
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The price of 3 chairs and 2 tables is 4500 rupees and price of 5 chairs and 3 tables is 7000 rupees, then find the price of 2 chairs and 2 tables.
answer:
Let the price of 1 chair be Rs x and the price of 1 table be Rs y. 
The price of 3 chairs and 2 tables is 4500 rupees
3x + 2y = 4500 ...(I)
Price of 5 chairs and 3 tables is 7000 rupees
5x + 3y = 7000 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 3 and (II) with 2
9x + 6y = 13500 ...(III)
10x + 6y = 14000 ...(IV)
subtracting IV from III
9x + 6y = 13500
10x + 6y = 14000
− − − 
−x = −500
∴ x = 500
Putting this value of x in I
3 × 500 + 2y = 4500
⇒ 1500 + 2y = 4500
⇒ 2y = 4500 - 1500 = 3000
⇒ y = 1500
Price of 2 chairs and 2 tables will be
= 2x + 2y
= 2(x + y)
= 2 (500 + 1500) 
= 2 × 2000
= 4000
Thus, the price of 2 chairs and 2 tables will be Rs 4000.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The Sum of the Digits in a Two-digits Number is 9. the Number Obtained by Interchanging the Digits Exceeds the Original Number by 27. Find the Two-digit Number.
answer:
Step 1: Write the conditions as equations

The sum of the digits is 9: x + y = 9.
The number obtained by interchanging the digits exceeds the original number by 27: 10y + x = 10x + y + 27.

Step 2: Simplify the second equation
10y + x = 10x + y + 27.
10y − y = 10x − x + 27,
9y = 9x + 27.
y = x + 3
Step 3: Solve the system of equations

x + y = 9,
y = x + 3

Substitute y = x + 3 into x + y = 9:
x + (x + 3) = 9
2x + 3 = 9
2x = 6 ⇒ x = 3
Substitute x = 3 into y = x + 3:
y = 3 + 3 = 6
10x + y = 10(3) + 6 = 36.
The two-digit number is 36.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: In Δ Abc, the Measure of Angle a is Equal to the Sum of the Measures of ∠ B and ∠ C. Also the Ratio of Measures of ∠ B and ∠ C is 4 : 5. Then Find the Measures of Angles of the Triangle.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Divide a Rope of Length 560 Cm into 2 Parts Such that Twice the Length of the Smaller Part is Equal to 1 3 of the Larger Part. Then Find the Length of the Larger Part.
answer:
Let the length of the smaller part of the rope be ‘x’ cm and that of the larger part be ‘y’ cm.
According to the first condition,
The total length of the rope is 560 cm.
∴ x + y = 560 ...(i)
Twice the length of the smaller part = 2x
1/3 rd length of the larger part = 1/3y
According to the second condition,
2x = 1/3y
∴ 2x × 3 = y
∴ 6x – y = 0 ...(ii)
Adding equations (i) and (ii),
x + y = 560
6x − y = 0
+__________
7x = 560
x = 560/7
∴ x = 80
Substituting x = 80 in equation (ii),
6x – y = 0
∴ 6(80) – y = 0
∴ 480 – y = 0
∴ y = 480
∴ The length of the larger part of the rope is 480 cm.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: In a competitive examination, there were 60 questions. The correct answer would carry 2 marks, and for incorrect answer 1 mark would be subtracted.
answer:
Let the number of correct answers be x and the number of incorrect answers be y. 
Total answers = Number of correct + number of incorrect =
x + y = 60 ...(I)
Total marks obtained = 90
2x - y = 90 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II) we have
x + y = 60
2y − y = 90
+ 
3x = 150
x = 150/3
⇒ x = 50
Substituting x = 50 in equation (I)
x + y = 60
50 + y = 60
y = 60 - 50
y = 10
Number of questions he got wrong is 10.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. x - 2y = -1 ; 2x - y = 7
answer:
x - 2y = -1 ...(I)
2x - y = 7 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 2,
2x - 4y = -2 ....(III)
Subtracting (III) from (II)
2x - y = 7
2x - 4y = -2
- + + 
3y = 9
∴ y = 3
Putting the value of y in (I) we get,
∴ x - 2y = -1
⇒ x - 2 × 3 = -1
⇒ x - 6 = -1
⇒ x = -1 + 6
⇒ x = 5
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. x + y = 11 ; 2x - 3y = 7
answer:
x + y = 11 ...(I)
2x - 3y = 7 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 3
3x + 3y = 33 ...(III)
Adding (II) and (III)
2x - 3y = 7
3x + 3y = 33
+ 
5x = 40
x = 40/5
⇒ x = 8
Putting the value of x in (I)
8 + y = 11
y = 11 - 8
⇒ y = 3
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. 2x + y = -2 ; 3x - y = 7
answer:
2x + y = -2 ...(I)
3x - y = 7 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II)
5x = 5
⇒ x = 1
Putting the value of x in (I)
2 × 1 + y = -2
⇒ 2 + y = -2
⇒ y = - 4
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. 2x - y = 5 ; 3x + 2y = 11
answer:
2x - y = 5 ...(I)
3x + 2y = 11 ...(II)
Multiplying (I) with 2 we get
4x - 2y = 10 ...(III)
Adding (II) with (III)
 3x + 2y = 11
+ 4x - 2y = 10
 7x = 21
⇒ x = 3
Putting the value of x in (I) we get
∴ 2x - y = 5 
⇒ 2 × 3 - y = 5
⇒ 6 - y = 5
⇒ y = 1
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. x - 2y = - 2 ; x + 2y = 10
answer:
x − 2y = −2 ...(I)
x + 2y = 10 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II)
 x − 2y = −2
+ x + 2y = 10 
 2x = 8
⇒ x = 4
Putting the value of x in (I) we get
∴ x - 2y = −2
⇒ 4 − 2y = −2
⇒ −2y = −6
⇒ y = 3
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: By equating coefficients of variables, solve the following equations. 3x - 4y = 7; 5x + 2y = 3
answer:
3x - 4y = 7 ...(I)
5x + 2y = 3 ...(II)
Multiply (II) with 2
10x + 4y = 6 ...(III)
Adding (I) and (III)
3x - 4y = 7
10x + 4y = 6 
13x = 13
⇒ x = 1
Putting the value of x in (I) we get
∴ 3x - 4y = 7
⇒ 3 × 1 - 4y = 7
⇒ 3 - 4y = 7
⇒ y = -1
Thus, x = 1 and y = -1
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: By equating coefficients of variables, solve the following equation. 5x + 7y = 17 ; 3x - 2y = 4
answer:
5x + 7y = 17 ...(I)
3x - 2y = 4 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 3 and (II) with 5
15x + 21y = 51 ...(III)
15x - 10y = 20 ...(IV)
Subtracting (IV) from (III) we get,
15x + 21y = 51
15x - 10y = 20 
- + - 
31y = 31
⇒ y = 1
Putting this value of y in (I) we get
∴ 5x + 7y = 17
5x + 7 × 1 = 17
⇒ 5x = 10
⇒ x = 2
Thus, x = 2, y = 1
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: By equating coefficients of variables, solve the following equation. x - 2y = -10 ; 3x - 5y = -12
answer:
x − 2y = −10 ...(I)
3x − 5y = −12 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 3
3x − 6y = −30 ...(III)
Subtracting (II) from (III) we get,
3x − 6y = −30
3x − 5y = −12 
- + + 
−y = −18
⇒ y = 18
Putting the value of y in (I) we get,
∴ x − 2y = −10
⇒ x − 2 × 18 = −10
⇒ x = −10 + 36 = 26
Thus, x = 26, y = 18
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: By equating coefficients of variables, solve the following equation. 4x + y = 34 ; x + 4y = 16
answer:
4x + y = 34 ...(I)
x + 4y = 16 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II) we get,
4x + y = 34
x + 4y = 16 
5x + 5y = 50
⇒ x + y = 10 ...(III)
Subtracting (II) from (I) we get,
4x + y = 34
x + 4y = 16 
- - - 
3x - 3y = 18
⇒ x - y = 6 ...(IV)
Adding (III) and (IV) we get,
x + y = 10
x - y = 6 
2x = 16
⇒ x = 8
Putting the value of x in (I) we get,
∴ 4x + y = 34
⇒ 4 (8) + y = 34
⇒ y = 34 - 32
⇒ y = 2
Thus, x = 8, y = 2
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. x3+y4=4;x2-y4=1
answer:
x/3 + y/4 = 4 ...(I)
x/2 - y/4 = 1 ...(II) 
Multiplying (I) with LCM of 3 and 4 which is 12, we get,
4x + 3y = 48 ...(III)
Multiplying (II) with LCM of 2 and 4 which is 4, we get,
2x - y = 4 ...(IV)
Multiplying (IV) with 2,
4x - 2y = 8 ...(V)
Subtracting (V) from (III)
4x + 3y = 48
4x - 2y = 8
- + - 
5y = 40
⇒ y = 8
Putting this value of y in (IV) we get,
∴ 2x - y = 4
⇒ 2x - 8 = 4
⇒ 2x = 12
⇒ x = 6.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. x3+5y=13;2x+y2=19
answer:
x/3 + 5y = 13 ...(I)
x + 15y = 13 × 3
∴ x + 15y = 39 ...(II)
2x + y/2 = 19 ...(III)
(4x+y)/2 = 19
4x + y = 19 × 2
∴ 4x + y = 38 ...(IV)
Multiplying (II) with 4 we get,
4x + 60y = 156 ...(V)
Subtracting (IV) from (V),
4x + 60y = 156
4x + y = 38 
- - - 
59y = 118
⇒ y = 2
Putting the value of y in (IV) we get,
∴ 4x + y = 38
⇒ 4x + 2 = 38
⇒ 4x = 36
⇒ x = 9
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Solve the following simultaneous equation. 2x+3y=13 ; 5x-4y=-2
answer:
2/x + 3/y = 13 ; 5/x - 4/y = -2
Let 1/x = u and 1/y = v
So, the equations obtained are
2u + 3v = 13 ...(I) (× 5)
5u - 4v = -2 ...(II) (× 2)
10u + 15v = 65 ...(III)
10u - 8v = - 4 ...(IV)
Subtracting (IV) from (III)
10u + 15v = 65
10u - 8v = - 4 
- + + 
23v = 69
⇒ v = 3
Putting the value of v in (I)
∴ 2u + 3v = 13
⇒ 2u + 3 × 3 = 13
⇒ 2u = 4
⇒ u = 2
1/x = u ⇒ x = 1/u = 1/2
1/y = v ⇒ y = 1/v = 1/3
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: A two digit number is 3 more than 4 times the sum of its digits. If 18 is added to this number, the sum is equal to the number obtained by interchanging the digits. Find the number.
answer:
1Show SolutionLet the two digit number be 10x + y. 
A two digit number is 3 more than 4 times the sum of its digits.
10x + y = 4 (x + y) + 3
⇒ 10x + y = 4x + 4y + 3
⇒ 2x − y = 1 ...(I)
If 18 is added to this number, the sum is equal to the number obtained by interchanging the digits.
10x + y + 18 = 10y + x
⇒ 9x − 9y = −18
⇒ x − y = −2 ...(II)
(I) − (II)
2x − y = 1
x − y = − 2 
− + + 
x = 3
Put x = 3 in equation (II)
∴ x − y = − 2
∴ 3 − y = − 2
∴ 3 + 2 = y
∴ y = 5
∴ The required number = 10x + y
= 10 × 3 + 5
= 30 + 5
= 35
Thus, the number obtained is 35.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The total cost of 8 books and 5 pens is 420 rupees and the total cost of 5 books and 8 pens is 321 rupees. Find the cost of 1 book and 2 pens.
answer:
Let the cost of 1 book be x and the cost of 1 pen be y. 
According to the first condition, the total cost of 8 books and 5 pens is 420 rupees.
∴ 8x + 5y = 420 ...(I)
According to the Second condition, the total cost of 5 books and 8 pens is 321 rupees.
5x + 8y = 321 ...(II)
Multiplying equation (I) by 5
40x + 25y = 2100 ...(III)
Multiplying equation (II) by 8
40x + 64y = 2568 ...(IV)
equation (IV) - (III)
 40x + 64y = 2568
- 40x + 25y = 2100
 39y = 468 
∴ y = 468/39
∴ y = 12
Putting the value of y = 12 in equation (I)
8x + 5y = 420 
⇒ 8x + 5 (12) = 420 
⇒ 8x + 60 = 420
⇒ 8x = 420 - 60
⇒ 8x = 360
⇒ x = 360/8
∴ x = 45
Cost of 1 book and 2 pens = x + 2y
= 45 + 2 (12)
= Rs. 69
∴ The Cost of 1 book and 2 pens = Rs. 69.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The ratio of incomes of two persons is 9 : 7. The ratio of their expenses is 4 : 3. Every person saves rupees 200, find the income of each.
answer:
Let the incomes of the two persons be x and y.
x/y = 9/7
⇒ 7x = 9y
⇒ 7x − 9y = 0 ...(I)
Savings of the two persons = Rs 200
Expenses = Income − Savings
(x − 200)/(y − 200) = 4/3
∴ 3(x − 200) = 4(y − 200)
⇒ 3x − 600 = 4y − 800
⇒ 3x − 4y = −200 ...(II)
Multiply (I) with 3 and (II) with 7.
21x − 27y = 0 ...(III)
21x − 28y = −1400 ...(IV)
(IV) − (III)
21x − 28y = −1400
21x − 27y = 0 
− + − 
−y = −1400
⇒ y = 1400
Putting the value y = 1400 in (I) we get
∴ 7x − 9y = 0
∴ 7x − 9(1400) = 0
∴ 7x − 12600 = 0
∴ 7x = 12600
∴ x = 12600/7
⇒ x = 1800
Thus, the income of the two persons is Rs 1800 and Rs 1400.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If the length of a rectangle is reduced by 5 units and its breadth is increased by 3 units, then the area of the rectangle is reduced by 9 square units.
answer:
Let the length of the rectangle be ‘x’ units and the breadth of the rectangle be ‘y’ units.
Area of the rectangle = xy sq. units
length of the rectangle is reduced by 5 units
∴ length = x – 5
breadth of the rectangle is increased by 3 units
∴ breadth = y + 3
area of the rectangle is reduced by 9 square units
∴ area of the rectangle = xy – 9
According to the first condition,
(x – 5) (y + 3) = xy – 9
∴ xy + 3x – 5y – 15 = xy – 9
∴ 3x – 5y = -9 + 15
∴ 3x – 5y = 6 ...(i)
length of the rectangle is reduced by 3 units
∴ length = x – 3
breadth of the rectangle is increased by 2 units
∴ breadth = y + 2
area of the rectangle is increased by 67 square units
∴ area of the rectangle = xy + 61
According to the second condition,
(x – 3) (y + 2) = xy + 67
∴ xy + 2x – 3y – 6 = xy + 67
∴ 2x – 3y = 67 + 6
∴ 2x – 3y = 73 ...(ii)
Multiplying equation (i) by 3,
9x – 15y = 18 ...(iii)
Multiplying equation (ii) by 5,
10x – 15y = 365 ...(iv)
Subtracting equation (iii) from (iv),
10x – 15y = 365
9x – 15y = 18 
- + - 
 x = 347
Substituting x = 347 in equation (ii),
2x – 3y = 73
∴ 2(347) – 3y = 73
∴ 694 – 73 = 3y
∴ 621 = 3y
∴ y = 621/3
∴ y = 207
∴ The length and breadth of rectangle are 347 units and 207 units respectively.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: If They Travel Towards Each Other They Will Meet After 1 Hour, Then Find Their Speeds.
answer:
Let the speed of the faster car be x km/h and that of the slower car be y km/h.
Total distance between them is 70 km.
Speed = "Distance" / "Time"
When both cars travel in the same direction, they meet in 7 hours. 
Total speed = x − y
x - y = 70/7
⇒ x - y = 10 ...(I)
When both cars move towards each other,
Total speed = x + y
x + y = 70/1
⇒ x + y = 70 ...(II)
Adding (I) and (II)
x - y = 10
x + y = 70 
2x = 80
⇒ x = 40
Putting the value of x in (I)
∴ x - y = 10
∴ 40 - y = 10
∴ y = 40 - 10
⇒ y = 30
∴ The speed of a car starting from A is 40 km/hr and the speed of a car starting from B is 30 km/hr.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: The sum of a two digit number and the number obtained by interchanging its digits is 99. Find the number.
answer:
Let x be the unit’s digit and y the tens digit.
∴ Two digit number = 10y + x
Number obtained on interchanging the digits = 10x + y. 
The sum of a two digit number and the number obtained by interchanging its digits is 99.
10x + y + 10y + x = 99
⇒ 11x + 11y = 99
Divide both sides by 11
⇒ x + y = 9
If x = 1, then y = 8,
If x = 2, then y = 7, etc.
If x = 3, then y = 6, and so on.
The number can be 18, 27, 36, etc.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Alka spends 90% of the money that she receives every month, and saves Rs. 120. How much money does she get monthly?
answer:
Let her monthly income be Rs 'x'
Savings = Rs 120
Expenditure = 90% of x = (90)/100 x 
Income = Expenditure + Savings
x = (90x)/100 + 120
x - 120 = (90x)/100
100 (x - 120) = 90x
100x - 12000 = 90x
100x - 90x = 12000
10x = 12000
x = 12000/10
x = 1200
Thus, total monthly salary = Rs 1200.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Sumit borrowed a capital of Rs. 50,000 to start his food products business. In the first year he suffered a loss of 20%. He invested the remaining capital in a new sweets business and made a profit
answer:
Sumit borrowed capital = Rs. 50000
Loss incurred in the first year = 20% × 50000
= 20/100 xx 50000 = ₹ 10,000
Remaining Capital = Total Capital − Loss
50,000 − 10,000
= ₹ 40,000
Profit in food products business = 5% of ₹ 40,000
= 5/100 xx 40000
= ₹ 2000
Capital held by Sumit after two years = 40,000 + 2000
= ₹ 42,000
Total loss in original capital = 50,000 − 42,000
= ₹ 8000
Loss% = 8000/50000 xx 100
= 16 %
Sumit suffered a total loss of 16% on the original capital.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Nikhil spent 5% of his monthly income on his children's education, invested 14% in shares, deposited 3% in a bank and used 40% for his daily expenses.
answer:
Let the monthly income be Rs x = 100/100x
Money spent on children's education = 5% "of" x = 5/100x
Money spent on shares = 14% "of" x = 14/100x
Money deposited in banks = 3% "of" x = 3/100x
Money spent on daily expenses = 40% "of" x = 40/100x
Savings = "Rs" 19,000
5/100x + 14/100x + 3/100x + 40/100x + 19000 = 100/100x
⇒ 62/100x + 19000 = 100/100x
⇒ 100/100x-62/100x = 19000
⇒ 38/100x = 19000
⇒ x = 19000 xx 100/38
⇒ x = 50000
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Mr. Sayyad kept Rs. 40,000 in a bank at 8% compound interest for 2 years. Mr. Fernandes invested Rs. 1,20,000 in a mutual fund for 2 years.
answer:
mount deposited by Mr. Sayyed = Rs 40,000
Rate of interest = 8%
Time = 2 years
CI = Amount - Principle
= "P"(1 + "R"/100)^"n" - "P"
= 40000 (1 + 8/100)² - 40000
= 40000 ((100 + 8)/100)² - 40000
= 40000 [ ( 108 / 100)² - 1]
= 40000 ( 1.1664 - 1 )
= 40000 xx 0.1664
= Rs. 6656
Percentage profit = 6656/40000 xx 100 = 16.64%
Investment in mutual funds by Mr. Fernandes = "Rs" 1,20,000
The amount Mr. Fernandes got at the end of 2 years from the mutual fund = "Rs" 1,92,000
Profit = 1,92,000 - 1,20,000 = "Rs". 72000
Percentage profit from mutual funds = 72000/120000 xx 100 = 60%
Thus, investment by Mr. Fernandes turned out to be more profitable.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Sameera spent 90% of her income and donated 3% for socially useful causes. If she left with Rs. 1750 at the end of the month, what was her actual income?
answer:
Let Sameera's actual income be Rs x.
Money spent = 90% of x =90/100 x
Money donated = 3% of x = 3/100x
Money left = Rs 1750
Money spent + money donated + money left = Total income
⇒ 90/100x + 3/100x + 1750 = 100/100x
⇒ 100/100x - 93/100x = 1750
⇒ 7/100x = 1750
⇒ 7x = 175000
⇒ x = 175000/7
⇒ x = 25000
Thus, Sameera's actual income is Rs 25000.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Observe the table given below. Check and decide, whether the individuals have to pay income tax. Individuals (i) Miss Nikita (ii) Mr.Kulkarni (iii) Miss Mehta (iv) Mr. Bajaj (v) Mr. Desilva
answer:
Miss Nikita is age 27, and her taxable income is Rs 2,34,000. She doesn't need to pay income tax as her earnings are less than Rs 2,50,000.
Mr. Kulkarni is of age 36 years and his taxable income is Rs 3,27,000. So, his taxable income falls in the slab of 2,50,001 to 5,00,000. Thus, he needs to pay the income tax.
Miss Mehta is of age 44 and has a taxable income of Rs 5,82,000. She needs to pay the income tax as her taxable income falls in the slab of Rs 5,00,001 to 10,00,000.
Mr. Bajaj is of age 64 years and has a taxable income of Rs 8,40,000. He needs to pay the income tax as his taxable income falls in the slab of Rs 5,00,001 to 10,00,000.
Mr. Desilva is of age 81 years and has a taxable income of Rs 4,50,000. He doesn't need to pay income tax as he is in the super senior category and has a taxable income of less than Rs 5,00,000.

Will have to pay income tax or not

(i)

he needs to pay the income tax.

Rs. 5,82,000
She needs to pay the income tax.

Rs. 8,40,000
He needs to pay the income tax.

subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Mr. Kartarsingh (age 48 years) works in a private company. His monthly income after deduction of allowances is Rs. 42,000 and every month he contributes Rs. 3000 to GPF.
answer:
Monthly Income = Rs. 42,000
Gross annual income = Rs. 42,000 × 12 = Rs. 5,04,000
Applicable deductions:
Monthly GPF contribution = Rs. 3000
Annual GPF contribution = Rs. 3000 × 12 = Rs. 36,000
NSC = Rs. 15,000
Donation in PM's relief fund = Rs. 12000
Total applicable deductions = Rs. 36,000 + 15,000 + 12,000 = Rs. 63,000
Total taxable income = Gross annual income - total applicable deductions 
= Rs. 5,04,000 - Rs. 63,000 = Rs. 4,41,000
Now the Total taxable income falls in the slab of 2,50,001 to 5,00,000. 
So, Income tax = 5% of (Taxable income - 2,50,000)
= 5% of (441000 − 250000)
= 5/100 xx 191000
= ₹ 9550
Education Cess = 2% of Income Tax
= 9550 × 2/100 '
= Rs. 191
Secondary and Higher Education Cess = 1% of Income Tax
= 9550 × 1/100
= Rs. 95.5
Total tax to be paid = Income tax + education cess + secondary and higher education cess
= 9550 + 191 + 95.5
= Rs. 9836.5
∴ Mr. Kartar Singh will have to pay 9836.50 income tax.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: For different types of investments what is the maximum permissible amount under section 80C of income tax?
answer: 1,50,000 rupees2,50,000 rupees1,00,000 rupees2,00,000 rupees
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Mr. Shekhar spends 60% of his income. From the balance he donates Rs. 300 to an orphanage. He is then left with Rs. 3,200. What is his income?
answer:
Let Mr. Shekhar's income be Rs x. 
Money spent = 60% of x = 60/100 x
Money donated in orphanage = Rs 300
Money left = Rs 3200
Money spent + Money donated + Money left = Total income
⇒ 60/100x + 300 + 3200 = x
⇒ (6x)/10 + 3500 = x
⇒ x - (6x)/10 = 3500
⇒ (10x - 6x)/10 = 3500
⇒ (4x )/10 = 3500
⇒ x = 8750
Thus, Mr. Shekhar's income is Rs 8750.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Mr. Hiralal invested Rs. 2,15,000 in a Mutual Fund. He got Rs. 3,05,000 after 2 years. Mr. Ramniklal invested Rs. 1,40,000 at 8% compound interest
answer:
Mr. Hiralal's investment = Rs. 2,15,000
Return of investment = Rs. 3,05,000
Profit = Mr. Hiralal's investment - Return of investment
= 3,05,000 - 2,15,000
= Rs. 90,000
Profit % = (90,000)/(2,15,000)xx 100
= 41.86%
For Ramaniklal, 
P = 140000, R = 8%, n = 2 years
Compound interest = I = P[(1 + R/100)ⁿ - 1]
= 140,000 [(1 + 8/100)² - 1]
= 140000 [(1 + 0.08)2 − 1]
= 140000 (1.1664 − 1)
= 140000 × 0.1664
= 23296
Profit% = (23296)/(140000) × 100 = 16.64%
Thus, Mr. Hiralal's investment is more profitable.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: At the start of a year there were Rs. 24,000 in a savings account. After adding Rs. 56,000 to this the entire amount was invested in the bank at 7.5% compound interest.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Mr. Manohar gave 20% part of his income to his elder son and 30% part to his younger son. He gave 10% of the balance as donation to a school.
answer:
Let Mr. Manohar's income be Rs x. 
Money given to elder son = 20% of x = ( 20 )/100x
Money given to younger son = 30% of x = ( 30)/100x
Balance = x - ((20x)/100 + (30x)/100)
= x - (50x)/100
= (100x - 50x)/100
= ₹ (50x)/100
10% of the remaining income is donated to the school.
Donation = (50x)/100 xx 10/100
= ₹ (5x)/100
Income − Expenditure = Savings
x - ((20x)/100 + (30x)/100 + (5x)/100) = 180000
∴ (100x)/100 - ((55x)/100) = 180000
∴ 100x − 55x = 180000 × 100
∴ 45x = 180000 × 100
∴ x = (180000 xx 100)/45
∴ x = 400000
Thus, Mr. Manohar's income is Rs. 4,00,000.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Kailash used to spend 85% of his income. When his income increased by 36% his expenses also increased by 40% of his earlier expenses. How much percentage of his earning he saves now ?
answer:
1Show SolutionLet the Kailas's income be ₹ x.
Kailash used to spend 85% of his income.
∴ Money spent = x xx 85/100
= ₹ (85x)/100
Income − Expenditure = Savings
x - (85x)/100 = Savings
∴ Savings = (15x)/100
Kailash's income increased by 36%.
∴ Kailash's increased income = x + x xx 36/100
= x + (36x)/100
= ₹ (136x)/100
Kailash expenses increased by 40%.
∴ Increased expenses of Kailash = (85x)/100 + (85x)/100 xx 40/100
= (85x)/100 + (34x)/100
= ₹ (119x)/100
Kailash's current savings = Income - Expenditure
= (136x)/100 - (119x)/100
= ₹ (17x)/100
Kailash's percentage of savings = "Savings"/"Income" xx 100
= (17x)/100 + (136x)/100 xx 100
= (17x)/100 + 100/(136x) xx 100
= 12.5
Kailas now saves 12.5 percent of his earnings.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Total income of Ramesh, Suresh, and Preeti are 8,07,000 rupees. The percentages of their expenses are 75%, 80%, and 90% respectively. If the ratio of their savings is 16: 17: 12
answer:
Let the savings of Ramesh, Suresh, and Preeti be 16x, 17x, and 12x. 
Expenditure of Ramesh = 75% of income
So, Savings of Ramesh = 25% of income
We know
Saving = Income − expenditure
16x = 25 % of income
⇒ 16x = 25/100 "(Income of Ramesh)"
⇒ 64x = (Income of Ramesh)
Similarly, Savings of Suresh = 20% of his income
So,
17x = 20% (Income of Suresh)
⇒ 17x = 20/100 "(Income of Suresh)"
⇒ 85x = (Income of Suresh)
Savings of Preeti = 10% of her Income
⇒ 12x = 10/100 "(Income of Preeti)"
⇒ 120x = (Income of Preeti)
Given that total income of Ramesh, Suresh and Preeti is 8,07,000.
64x + 85x + 120x = 807000
⇒ 269x = 807000
⇒ x = Rs. 3000.
Savings of Ramesh = Rs 16 × 3000 = Rs. 48000
Savings of Suresh = Rs 17 × 3000 = Rs. 51000
Savings of Preeti = Rs 12 × 3000 = Rs. 36000.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Compute the income tax payable by following individual. Mr. Kadam who is 35 years old and has a taxable income of Rs. 13,35,000.
answer:
Taxable income = Rs. 13,35,000
Income tax = Rs. 1,12,500 + 30% (Rs. 13,35,000 – Rs. 10,00,000)
= Rs. 1,12,500 + 30/100 xx 335000
= Rs. 1,12,500 + Rs. 1,00,500
= Rs. 2,13,000
Education Cess = 2% of income tax 
= Rs. 2,13,000 xx2/100 = Rs. 4,260
And Secondary and Higher Secondary Education Cess = 1%
= Rs. 2,13,000 xx1/100 = Rs. 2,130
Total income tax = Rs. 2,13,000 + Rs. 4,260 + Rs. 2,130
= Rs. 2,19,390
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Compute the income tax payable by following individual. Mr. Khan is 65 years of age and his taxable income is Rs. 4,50,000.
answer:
Taxable income = Rs. 4,50,000
Income tax = 5% (Rs. 4,50,000 - Rs. 3,00,000)
= 5/100 xx 150,000
= Rs. 7500
Education cess = 2% of income tax
= 2/100 xx 7500 = Rs. 150
Secondary and Higher Education cess = 1% of income tax
= 1/100 xx 7500 = 75
Total income tax = Income tax + Education cess + Secondary and higher education cess
= 7500 + 150 + 75 = Rs. 7725
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1

question: Compute the income tax payable by following individual. Miss Varsha (Age 26 years) has a taxable income of Rs. 2,30,000.
answer:
Taxable income = Rs 2,30,000 age = 26 years
The yearly income of Miss Varsha is less than Rs 2,50,000.
Hence, Miss Varsha will not have to pay income tax.
subject: Algebra 9th Mathematics 1
